Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}{{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |例祭={{jalink|October 14|Q2922}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}}, {{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}, {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, {{jalink|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}{{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |例祭={{jalink|October 14|Q2922}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}}, {{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}, {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, {{jalink|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} * {{jalink|Harutaro Iizuka|Q85271286}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしやきりゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kōzuke Province]] [[Category:群馬県の神社]] [[Category:桐生市の歴史]] [[Category:桐生市の建築物]] [[Category:上野国の式内社]] [[Category:群馬県の旧郷社]] [[Category:加茂神社]] [[Category:群馬県指定天然記念物]] [[Category:群馬県内の市町村指定有形文化財]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}{{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |例祭={{jalink|October 14|Q2922}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}}, {{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}, {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, {{jalink|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}{{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |例祭={{jalink|October 14|Q2922}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}}, {{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}, {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, {{jalink|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}{{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}{{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}} |例祭={{jalink|October 14|Q2922}} |本殿={{jalink|nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501}}, {{jalink|Kiryu|Q634022}}, {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture|Q129499}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|shrine ranking system}} it was a {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{jalink|Emperor Sujin|Q333530}}, {{jalink|Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{jalink|Yamashiro Province|Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{jalink|Enryaku|Q1373176}} 15 ({{jalink|796|Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{jalink|kansha}} together with {{jalink|Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|Miwa Shrine}} (''{{jalink|Nihon Koki|Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{jalink|Gangyo|Q747451}} 4 ({{jalink|880|Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{jalink|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{jalink|Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{jalink|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{jalink|Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{jalink|Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613}} departed for the {{jalink|Later Three Years' War|Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{jalink|kagura|Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{jalink|bettōji|Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{jalink|hanging Buddha|Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{jalink|Muromachi period|Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{jalink|Pacific War|Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{jalink|air raids|Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{jalink|shintai|Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{jalink|1945|Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{jalink|Japanese rat snake|Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{jalink|messenger deity|Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{jalink|rikutō|Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{jalink|Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||Natural monument}} ** {{jalink|fir|Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{jalink|Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{jalink|Hikobe family residence|Q11487959}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352}} * {{jalink|Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263}} ({{jalink|National Route 50|Q2912151}} / {{jalink|National Route 122|Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{jalink|List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308}} * {{jalink|Harutaro Iizuka|Q85271286}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしやきりゆうし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamo Shrine (Kiryu)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501|WD=Q11485501}}, {{ill|lt=Kiryu|Q634022|WD=Q634022}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 15 ({{ill|lt=796|Q33452|WD=Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{ill||lt=kansha|UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|WD=Q11608848|lt=Miwa Shrine}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Koki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 4 ({{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|WD=Q84978067|lt=Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|WD=Q11463293|lt=Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{ill|lt=Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|WD=Q8047593|lt=Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613}} departed for the {{ill|lt=Later Three Years' War|Q1187411|WD=Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{ill|lt=kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{ill|lt=hanging Buddha|Q108461401|WD=Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{ill|lt=air raids|Q2380335|WD=Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{ill|lt=Japanese rat snake|Q2562020|WD=Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{ill|lt=messenger deity|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{ill|lt=rikutō|Q3434007|WD=Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||WD=|lt=Natural monument}} ** {{ill|lt=fir|Q1718147|WD=Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Hikobe family residence|Q11487959|WD=Q11487959}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352|WD=Q11537352}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263|WD=Q11537263}} ({{ill|lt=National Route 50|Q2912151|WD=Q2912151}} / {{ill|lt=National Route 122|Q6156748|WD=Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{ill|lt=rikutō|Q3434007|WD=Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||WD=|lt=Natural monument}} ** {{ill|lt=fir|Q1718147|WD=Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Hikobe family residence|Q11487959|WD=Q11487959}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352|WD=Q11537352}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263|WD=Q11537263}} ({{ill|lt=National Route 50|Q2912151|WD=Q2912151}} / {{ill|lt=National Route 122|Q6156748|WD=Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg {{commons category|Category:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} [[JA:賀茂神社 (桐生市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11634952}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kamo Shrine |画像=[[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) |所在地={{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}{{ill|lt=Kiryu|Q634022|WD=Q634022}}{{ill|lt=Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501|WD=Q11485501}} 6-833 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=(tradition) in the reign of the 10th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}} |例祭={{ill|lt=October 14|Q2922|WD=Q2922}} |本殿={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} with copper-sheet roof |神事=Otabi ritual |地図= {{Location map|Japan Gunma|label=Kamo Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] '''Kamo Shrine''' (かもじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Hirosawa-cho|Q11485501|WD=Q11485501}}, {{ill|lt=Kiryu|Q634022|WD=Q634022}}, {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture|Q129499|WD=Q129499}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=shrine ranking system}} it was a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-Mikoto|Q11634943|WD=Q11634943|lt=Kamo Wakeikazuchi-no-kami}} === History === Tradition says that in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|WD=Q333530}}, {{ill|lt=Toyoki Iribiko no Mikoto|Q11633375|WD=Q11633375}} invited a Kamo deity from {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|WD=Q749276}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=200-202}}. In {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176}} 15 ({{ill|lt=796|Q33452|WD=Q33452}}), it was listed as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kansha|1=UNKNOWN}} together with {{ill|lt=Miwa Shrine (Kiryu)|Q11608848|WD=Q11608848|lt=Miwa Shrine}} (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Koki|Q1077029|WD=Q1077029}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In {{ill|lt=Gangyo|Q747451|WD=Q747451}} 4 ({{ill|lt=880|Q23618|WD=Q23618}}), along with Miwa Shrine, {{ill|lt=Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine|Q84978067|WD=Q84978067|lt=Kohanoyado Hokora Shrine}}, and {{ill|lt=Kohori Shrine|Q11463293|WD=Q11463293|lt=Kohori Shrine}}, it was granted Senior Fifth Rank Lower, Merit Grade 12 (''{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|lt=Sandai Jitsuroku}}''){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the Engishiki register it appears as Kamo Shrine, small shrine, Yamada District ({{ill|lt=Yamada District (Gunma)|Q8047593|WD=Q8047593|lt=Yamada District}}){{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Editorial Committee|1958|p=187}}. In the ''Kozuke no Kuni Jinmyocho'', it appears as "Junior First Rank Kamo Daimyojin"{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. There is a tradition that when {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yoshiie|Q32613|WD=Q32613}} departed for the {{ill|lt=Later Three Years' War|Q1187411|WD=Q1187411}}, he prayed at this shrine and performed {{ill|lt=kagura|Q1348260|WD=Q1348260}} on his triumphant return{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200,1086-1087}}. Adjacent Hōraku-ji was formerly this shrine's {{ill|lt=bettōji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}; a wooden carved {{ill|lt=hanging Buddha|Q108461401|WD=Q108461401}} preserved there (Kiryu City designated important cultural property) is thought to have been this shrine's principal icon from the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845}}{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=389-390}}{{Cite web |title=Hōraku-ji principal icon |url=https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002087.html |website=www.city.kiryu.lg.jp |access-date=2024-08-20}}. In Meiji 5 (1872), it was listed as a gōsha{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=198-200}}. Late in the {{ill|lt=Pacific War|Q184425|WD=Q184425}}, due to intensifying {{ill|lt=air raids|Q2380335|WD=Q2380335}}, a cave shelter was dug behind the shrine to protect the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|WD=Q327532}} and worshipers; on May 24, {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|WD=Q5240}} (Showa 20), the sacred objects and treasures were transferred there{{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1961-12-25 |year=1961 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024355 |pages=268-270 |volume=Lower volume}}{{要登録}}. === Legends === A tailless {{ill|lt=Japanese rat snake|Q2562020|WD=Q2562020}} is regarded as this shrine's {{ill|lt=messenger deity|Q10444973|WD=Q10444973}}, and killing one is said to bring a curse{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. The deity is said to dislike white horses, so parishioners do not keep them. It is said that even passing in front of the shrine while leading a white horse caused curses{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said to avoid blacksmithing and barrel-making, because the sounds are disliked by the deity{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|p=1086}}. Parishioners are also said not to grow upland rice ({{ill|lt=rikutō|Q3434007|WD=Q3434007}}), because either it would not bear grain or misfortune would occur{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=1073-1074}}. === Precinct === File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), honden.jpg|Main hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), tourou.jpg|Stone lantern (Kiryu City designated cultural property) File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), mikoshisha and kaguraden.jpg|Mikoshi storehouse / Kagura hall File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), momi.jpg|Fir tree (Gunma Prefecture designated natural monument) === Subsidiary shrines === * Shimokamo Shrine * Katori Shrine * Kashima Shrine * Yasaka Shrine * Toyoki Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-1.jpg|Kamo-Mioya Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-3.jpg|Katori Shrine / Kashima Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-2.jpg|Yasaka Shrine File:Kamo-jinja (Kiryu), keidaisha-4.jpg|Toyoki Shrine === Cultural properties === === Gunma Prefecture designated cultural properties === * {{ill|lt=Gunma Prefecture designated natural monuments||WD=|lt=Natural monument}} ** {{ill|lt=fir|Q1718147|WD=Q1718147}} grove of Kamo Shrine - designated March 27, 2008 (Heisei 20)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/kenshitei/1001960.html Fir grove of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. === Kiryu City designated cultural properties === * Important cultural property (tangible cultural property) ** Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine - made in 1378 (Eiwa 4), {{ill|lt=Nanboku-cho period (Japan)|Q264012|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-cho period}}. Designated March 14, 1996 (Heisei 8)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/yuukei/1002036.html Stone lantern of Kamo Shrine] (Kiryu City website).. * Intangible folk cultural properties ** Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura - designated January 21, 1974 (Showa 49)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002098.html Kamo Shrine Dada Kagura] (Kiryu City website).. ** Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual - designated April 11, 1991 (Heisei 3)[https://www.city.kiryu.lg.jp/kankou/bunkazai/1010700/shishitei/mukei/1002097.html Kamo Shrine Otabi ritual] (Kiryu City website).. Held on the night of Setsubun. A rope boundary is set in the precinct to form an otabi fire ground, brushwood is piled and lit, and parishioners divide into two groups carrying burning bundles and throw sacred fire at each other three times with shouted calls{{Sfn|Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee|1971|pp=203-204}}. === Nearby === * {{ill|lt=Hikobe family residence|Q11487959|WD=Q11487959}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Automobile Museum|Q11537352|WD=Q11537352}} * {{ill|lt=Kiryu Bypass|Q11537263|WD=Q11537263}} ({{ill|lt=National Route 50|Q2912151|WD=Q2912151}} / {{ill|lt=National Route 122|Q6156748|WD=Q6156748}}) === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1958-8-15 |year=1958 |publisher=Kiryu City History Publishing Committee |ref=harv |author=Kiryu City History Editorial Committee |doi=10.11501/3024325 |volume=Upper volume}}{{要登録}} * {{Cite book|和書 |title=Kiryu City History |date=1971-3-1 |year=1971 |publisher=Kiryu City Hall |ref=harv |editor=Kiryu City History Supplementary Volume Editorial Committee |volume=Supplementary volume |doi=10.11501/3021305}}{{要登録}} === See also === {{Commonscat|Kamo-jinja (Kiryu)}} * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Kozuke Province|Q11360308|WD=Q11360308}} * {{ill|lt=Harutaro Iizuka|Q85271286|WD=Q85271286}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもしんしやきりゆうし}} ==
